page: Vultr voc-s-24c-192gb-5760s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with 24 vCPUs and 24 physical cores based on the x86_64 AMD EPYC-Rome processor architecture. Operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz, this server features a shared CPU allocation model. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled and consists of a 5760 GB NVMe SSD, while graphics acceleration is absent with zero GPUs.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong performance characteristics. Both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests place the instance in the top 25% of tested hardware, a tier also matched by its Passmark CPU and physics scores. Memory performance is particularly notable, with large-block read and write bandwidth benchmarks reaching top-tier status in the top 10% of results. Other operations, including database transactions, Redis SET operations, static web serving throughput, and single-threaded gzip compression, perform within the average range.

The server offers high resource density, combining a large memory footprint with substantial local NVMe storage. However, the shared CPU allocation model presents a potential tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, exclusive CPU performance. Given its 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and massive local storage, this configuration is well-suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, large-scale web serving, and data processing tasks that benefit from fast local read and write speeds.
description: Vultr voc-s-24c-192gb-5760s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server powered by a 24-core AMD EPYC-Rome processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features a shared CPU allocation running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 5760 GB NVMe SSD. While it lacks GPU acceleration, the server delivers strong CPU and memory performance, with large-block memory read and write bandwidth benchmarks reaching the top 10% of tested systems. The 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and high-capacity local NVMe storage make this instance highly efficient for memory-intensive applications. It is best suited for hosting large databases, caching layers, data processing pipelines, and high-throughput web servers that require substantial local storage and high memory capacity without requiring dedicated CPU cores.
og_description: Vultr voc-s-24c-192gb-5760s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 24 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 5760 GB NVMe storage, delivering strong memory bandwidth and database performance.
meta_description: Vultr voc-s-24c-192gb-5760s-amd is an AMD EPYC-powered server with 24 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 5760 GB NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity memory and NVMe storage server designed for database hosting, caching layers, and intensive data processing workloads.
bullet_points: 24 AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz with shared CPU allocation
192.0 GB of system memory providing an 8.0 GB per core ratio
5760 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier memory read and write bandwidth performance in the top 10%
Optimized for large databases, Redis caching, and data processing
